title: SuxOS residential-egress contract (sux ↔ suxrouter)
description: Slice-6 spike (docs/design/2026-07-16-suxos-vx-next-arc.md §5; docs/design/2026-07-16-residential-egress-contract.md). The seam between sux's edge/Worker code and suxrouter's rpcd/ucode firmware, extracted out of prose design docs (ideal-router-image.md, router-watchdog.md, home-node-connectivity.md live in sux) into one typed, conformance-checkable place. This is the schema ONLY — each side's CI validates its own surface against it; see scripts/contracts/*.stub.sh for the two conformance-check stubs this spike hands off. Fields marked TODO need the actual values pulled from the prose docs in a follow-up PR that also wires enforcement in both repos' CI.

endpoints.description: Every HTTP(S) surface suxrouter's rpcd exposes that sux's edge calls, or vice versa.

auth.description: TODO: fill from home-node-connectivity.md — the HMAC scheme (header name, signed fields, clock-skew tolerance) that authenticates calls across the seam.

ssrf.description: TODO: fill from ideal-router-image.md — the pass-through rules that keep the router from being turned into an open SSRF relay.

deniedTargets.description: CIDR ranges / hostnames the router must never proxy to (link-local, cloud metadata IPs, etc).

hostAllowlist.description: TODO: fill from router-watchdog.md — the hosts the router is permitted to reach or forward for on behalf of sux.

statusSemantics.description: Expected HTTP status codes and what each means across the seam, so both sides agree on error handling (this is the class that already produced the proxy empty-body incident).
